title: Maharanga thomsonii
taxonomy: Maharanga thomsonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Maharanga. Maharanga thomsonii is a type of flowering plant native to the Himalayas. The classification of Maharanga thomsonii includes its family, genus, and species levels. It is further classified based on its physical characteristics, growth habits, and other morphological features.
uses: Maharanga thomsonii has been traditionally used in various parts of the Himalayas for its medicinal properties. Its extracts and roots have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive issues. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. More studies are required to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Maharanga thomsonii.

preparations: Maharanga thomsonii is reportedly used in traditional medicine in various parts of Asia, particularly in India and Nepal.
